criteria.requirement: The layout establishes one clear visual hierarchy: the primary content dominates, secondary elements support it, and spacing groups related things together.
criteria.pass: A first glance lands on the element the brief names as primary; groups read as groups, with consistent alignment and deliberate breathing room between sections.
criteria.fail: Elements compete for the same first glance, alignment drifts between sections, or unrelated items sit closer together than related ones.
positive_anchors: The dominant element on each required viewport is the one the brief names as primary.
Consistent gutters and margins hold across sections at both required viewports.
Whitespace separates concerns — nothing important is crowded against an edge or a neighbor.
negative_anchors: Two blocks of equal visual weight fight over the top of the page.
A label or caption sits nearer to the wrong element than to its own.
criteria.insufficient_evidence: Both required viewport captures are needed; a single cropped screenshot cannot show hierarchy across the whole surface.

criteria.requirement: Type is legible and deliberately scaled: one recognizable typographic scale, readable sizes and line lengths, no clipped or overflowing text.
criteria.pass: Headings, body and labels sit on one consistent scale; every line of text is readable at both required viewports with no unintended truncation or wrap.
criteria.fail: Text clips, overflows its container, or wraps mid-word; sizes vary without pattern; body copy is too small or its lines too long to read comfortably.
positive_anchors: Heading levels step down a visible, consistent scale.
Body text holds a comfortable measure on both the desktop and the mobile capture.
negative_anchors: A heading or button label is cut off by its container at either viewport.
Two elements of the same rank carry visibly different sizes for no stated reason.
criteria.insufficient_evidence: Captures at both required viewports plus the DOM text are needed to distinguish a rendering clip from an authored abbreviation.

criteria.requirement: The palette is coherent and text stays readable against its background: sufficient contrast for content, deliberate use of accent color, no unreadable overlays.
criteria.pass: Content text is plainly readable against every background it sits on, and accent color marks the same class of thing wherever it appears.
criteria.fail: Text blends into its background, an overlay makes content beneath it illegible, or accent color is scattered without a discernible rule.
positive_anchors: Body and heading text read clearly on every panel of both viewport captures.
Interactive elements share one accent treatment that inert elements never borrow.
negative_anchors: Light text over a light image region with no scrim behind it.
The same accent color marks a link in one section and plain decoration in another.
criteria.insufficient_evidence: Full-color viewport captures are required; a grayscale or heavily compressed image cannot substantiate a contrast grade.

criteria.requirement: Interactive elements are discoverable and answer the user: controls look actionable, and an action produces a visible response.
criteria.pass: What is clickable looks clickable, and the film shows each exercised control responding — state change, navigation, or visible acknowledgment.
criteria.fail: Controls are indistinguishable from inert content, or an exercised action produces no visible response in the film or keyframes.
positive_anchors: The interaction film shows a control visibly acknowledging the exercised action.
Actionable elements share an affordance the inert content does not.
negative_anchors: A click in the film lands and nothing on the surface changes.
The console log records an error at the moment an interaction was exercised.
criteria.insufficient_evidence: The interaction film and the console log are needed; static screenshots alone cannot show a response to an action.

criteria.requirement: Motion is purposeful and continuous: transitions read as one movement, nothing flashes, jumps or reflows without cause.
criteria.pass: Across the film and its labeled keyframes, transitions carry the eye from one state to the next; content settles without flicker or layout jumps.
criteria.fail: The film shows a flash of unstyled or shifting content, an animation that stutters or strands mid-state, or a scroll that tears the layout.
positive_anchors: Adjacent keyframes read as moments of one continuous movement.
The surface reaches its settled state in the film without visible reflow.
negative_anchors: Content visibly jumps as late elements land and push the layout around.
A transition begins in one keyframe and never completes in any later one.
criteria.insufficient_evidence: The film and its labeled keyframes are needed; motion cannot be graded from still viewport captures alone.

criteria.requirement: The rendered surface delivers what the criterion row demands: the named content, structure and reference intent are visibly present, not approximated.
criteria.pass: Everything the row requires is visible in the evidence, where the row says it belongs, matching any named reference in intent.
criteria.fail: A required element is missing, placeholder content stands where real content was promised, or the surface visibly contradicts a named reference.
positive_anchors: Each element the row names can be pointed to in a capture.
The surface agrees with the named reference on layout intent, not just on rough content.
negative_anchors: Lorem-ipsum or template filler where the row promised real content.
The reference shows a structure the rendered surface never attempts.
criteria.insufficient_evidence: The criterion row, both viewport captures, and any named reference file are needed to compare the promise against the pixels.
